release_notes:
Alpha 4.0.10.50 - 2026-05-13 - SAP header matching: literal table
+ intelligent fuzzy fallback. Pablo follow-up on 4.0.10.49.

4.0.10.49 widened the literal HEADER_SYNONYMS table to cover the
three Kenvue Hellas EL label variants Pablo reported. Pablo
pointed out that a closed list is still brittle: any future SAP
locale with a slightly different label ("Taxable Amount",
"Output VAT", "Deductible VAT" ...) would still bring back the
0.00 cascade. Quote: "TIENES QUE PERMITIR FLEXIBILIDAD Y A LA
TOOL UN ESCANEO INTELIGENTE".

Fix: new ``_fuzzy_canonical_header`` in ``src/sap_parser.py``,
called ONLY when the literal synonym lookup misses. It
classifies the three SAP amount columns from a token vocabulary
of domain words:

- NET base    <-  "base" + tax token, OR a strong solo NET
word ("taxable", "imponible", "imponibile",
"imposable", "steuerbasis",
"steuerbasisbetrag")

- Output VAT  <-  "output" / "outgoing" / "sales" / "collected"
+ tax token, OR bare "tax payable" /
"VAT due"

- Input VAT   <-  "input" / "deduct" / "deductible" /
"recoverable" / "purchase" + tax token

False-positive guards:
- Any "rate" or "%" in the label vetoes amount classification.
- "Base" without a tax token AND without a strong solo word
returns None (so "Cash Discount Base" / "Reverse Charge
Base" don't silently land in the NET slot).
- Input wins over output when both signals coexist (e.g.
"Input Tax Payable" -> input VAT, not output).

100% backward compatible: the literal HEADER_SYNONYMS lookup
still runs first, so every header that resolved before keeps
resolving exactly the same way. Fuzzy is pure fallback.

Tests: +15 in tests/test_410_50_fuzzy_header_matching.py
covering NET base variants ("Taxable Amount", "VAT base",
"Imponible", "Steuerbasisbetrag"), Output VAT variants ("Output
VAT", "Sales Tax", "VAT Collected", bare "Tax Payable"), Input
VAT variants ("Input VAT", "Deductible VAT", "Recoverable VAT",
"Purchase Tax"), input-wins-over-output priority, the rate-
column false-positive guard, the non-tax-base false-positive
guard, an end-to-end parse of a workbook with never-enumerated
labels, and the literal-match-still-wins invariant.

Suite: 714 passed, 5 skipped, 0 failures.
